channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: Nvidia to invest up to $3.2 billion in Corning as part of massive optical fiber deal with 3 new factories focused on AI
item.description: Corning is opening three new advanced manufacturing plants in the U.S. dedicated entirely to optical technologies for Nvidia.

item.title: Apollo CEO Rowan warns of market correction, slams ‘egregious’ practices at rival insurers
item.description: Apollo CEO Marc Rowan warned that markets face an elevated risk of unexpected shocks, saying he is positioning the firm defensively for an expected correction.

item.title: FanDuel CEO Amy Howe is out after five years at the sportsbook
item.description: Howe has overseen FanDuel during a time of dramatic expansion in sports gambling and as prediction markets have exploded in popularity.

item.title: Dow rises 600 points as oil retreats on Iran deal progress; AMD jumps after earnings: Live updates
item.description: Buoyed by solid earnings and lower oil prices, the S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite both reached new intraday and closing highs on Tuesday.

item.title: This is what it costs investors to stay in cash — and what to do instead
item.description: A recent analysis by BlackRock shows how much income investors are leaving on the table with cash compared to bonds.

item.title: AMD's Su explains what's behind massive forecast change as stock roars 17% on earnings
item.description: CEO Lisa Su highlighted the growing demand for central processing units, doubling her long-term forecast after strong first-quarter earnings.

item.title: Elon Musk's Terafab chip factory in Texas could cost up to $119 billion, filing shows
item.description: Elon Musk's giant Terafab facility in Texas could cost up to $119 billion, with hopes of manufacturing chips for Tesla, SpaceX and xAI

item.title: Anthropic, SpaceX announce compute deal that includes space development
item.description: Anthropic has signed a deal with SpaceX, which owns rival xAI, to use all of the compute capacity at the company’s Colossus 1 data center in Memphis, Tennessee.

item.title: Goodbye quarterly earnings? Here's when traders believe this big change will happen
item.description: Traders are evenly divided as to whether the change will happen by Jan. 1. That would be an unusually fast timeline for the commission.

item.title: Marco Rubio heads to the Vatican as 2028 presidential buzz ramps up
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio is a frontrunner to succeed President Donald Trump in 2028, though Vice President JD Vance is also seen as a potential heir.

item.title: Ted Turner, outspoken founder of CNN, dies at 87
item.description: Ted Turner, the brash sportsman and entrepreneur whose ambition and instincts led to a media empire that included groundbreaking news network CNN, has died.

item.title: Berkshire-owned distribution giant to deploy driverless big rigs across U.S. Sun Belt
item.description: Berkshire Hathaway-owned McLane, one of America's biggest distribution companies, is deploying self-driving big rigs with Aurora for its restaurants business.

item.title: Trump says Iran will be bombed at a 'much higher level' if it doesn't agree to peace deal
item.description: Stocks rose and oil prices plunged on news reports signaling the U.S. and Iran could soon strike an agreement to end the war.

item.title: Earnings season is almost over. Morgan Stanley has some picks to play the end
item.description: Morgan Stanley sees stocks including Ulta Beauty and Target as potential winners to wrap up earnings season.

item.title: Surging gas prices are hitting lower-income households harder, New York Fed study shows
item.description: Lower-income consumers are compensating for higher gas prices by buying less.

item.title: Even if an Iran deal calms energy markets, one oil stock can still stand out
item.description: Shell plc (SHEL), along with other large integrated oil companies, have benefited from higher oil prices and should warrant investor attention.

item.title: Airlines spent 56.4% more on jet fuel in month after Iran war started, U.S. government says
item.description: U.S. airlines spent 56.4% more on jet fuel in March than they did in February, U.S. government data released Wednesday shows.

item.title: Uber and Disney are seeing the same remarkable dynamic in this economy. Both stocks are surging
item.description: Uber and Disney pointed to a resilient spending backdrop, with consumers continuing to shell out for rides, food delivery, vacations and theme park trips.

item.title: Why Boeing and Honeywell are rallying — plus, the AI trade gets another boost
item.description: Every weekday, the Investing Club releases the Homestretch; an actionable afternoon update just in time for the last hour of trading.

item.title: Novo Nordisk CEO says the drugmaker is more active than ever in seeking out deals
item.description: Novo Nordisk CEO Mike Doustdar says the Ozempic and Wegovy maker is looking for deals more than ever before.

item.title: Epstein files: Commerce Sec. Howard Lutnick questioned by House Oversight panel
item.description: Howard Lutnick admitted to a Senate committee in February that he and his family visited Epstein's private island in the U.S. Virgin Islands in 2012.

item.title: Ending soon: All-time high Marriott card bonus offers worth up to 200,000 points
item.description: These Marriott Bonvoy credit card welcome bonuses are at all-time highs, but they won't be for much longer.

item.title: Oil prices fall more than 7% as U.S. and Iran appear close to deal to end war
item.description: Oil prices fell sharply after Axios reported that the U.S. and Iran were closing in on an agreement to bring an end to the conflict.

item.title: Ken Griffin: Citadel expanding in Miami in response to NYC mayor Mamdani's 'poor taste' tax video
item.description: Mamdani's office said in response to Griffin, "our tax system is fundamentally broken. It rewards extreme wealth while working people are pushed to the brink."

item.title: Yankees legend Mariano Rivera says he supports an MLB salary cap
item.description: Former New York Yankee Mariano Rivera says he supports MLB salary cap as long as small-market teams are also spending.

item.title: Private payrolls rose by 109,000 in April, topping expectations, ADP says
item.description: The report provided more evidence of a stable labor market and less incentive for the Fed to lower interest rates.

item.title: Car payments squeeze Americans as auto debt hits $1.68 trillion, report finds
item.description: Auto debt has swelled to $1.68 trillion, a new analysis finds. Americans face costlier vehicles, higher interest rates and lengthier loan terms.

item.title: Supreme Court declines to pause order holding Apple in contempt in Epic Games lawsuit
item.description: Justice Elena Kagan declined to pause an appeals court ruling that deemed Apple in contempt in the Epic lawsuit contesting App Store fees.

item.title: Disney pops 7% after streaming, parks drive revenue beat in first report under CEO Josh D'Amaro
item.description: Disney revenue topped analyst expectations when the media giant reported its fiscal second-quarter earnings on Wednesday.

item.title: Apple's R&D investments top 10% of sales as AI race creates 'sense of urgency'
item.description: Apple’s R&D spending has crossed 10% of revenue for the first time in at least 30 years, and increased twice as fast as sales in the latest quarter.
